Container.Name: retro-vax-bbs
Container.Overview: Self-hosted SSH multi-user BBS with a retro DEC VAX/VMS terminal aesthetic. Runs two separate SSH listeners — one for regular users, one admin-only — so you can safely expose the public listener while keeping the admin listener behind your own VPN/VLAN. See the README and admin-guide (docs/admin-guide.md) in the project repo for full documentation before exposing the admin port to anything.

Config.Name: Admin SSH Port
Config.Description: Admin listener — admin accounts only, never forward this to the internet. In the default Bridge network mode, restrict who can reach this port by binding its host-side IP here to a VPN/VLAN-only address (e.g. your Tailscale IP) instead of leaving it open on all interfaces — do not rely on the ADMIN_HOST variable below to do this for you in Bridge mode.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent SQLite database and SSH host key. Required — the container will crash on startup without a writable directory mounted here. Back this up before any image upgrade.

Config.Name: SSH_HOST
Config.Description: Public listener bind address inside the container. Leave at 0.0.0.0 so it's reachable from outside the container.

Config.Name: ADMIN_HOST
Config.Description: Admin listener bind address inside the container. Leave at 0.0.0.0 in the default Bridge network mode — this is required for connectivity, not security: the app's own default of 'localhost' binds to the container's own loopback interface, which Bridge-mode port forwarding cannot reach, causing connection resets on login (often misreported as a failed key exchange). It provides NO access restriction by itself in this mode — the container can't see your host's VPN/VLAN interfaces from inside its own network namespace, so the actual restriction comes entirely from the host-IP field on the Admin SSH Port mapping above (scope it to a VPN/VLAN-only IP, never leave it open on all interfaces). Only change this to a specific VPN interface IP if you've switched this container to Host network mode (advanced view, network type) — in that mode it behaves exactly like a bare-metal install, and leaving this at 0.0.0.0 would then be genuinely unsafe.

Config.Name: REGISTRATION_MODE
Config.Description: Account registration mode: closed (admin-provisioned accounts only), invite-only, or open-with-approval.

Config.Name: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME
Config.Description: Optional: username for an admin account created automatically on first boot, instead of using the Console/exec workaround (this image has no shell, so Unraid's Console button won't work here). Must be set together with BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD below, or both left blank. Only takes effect while the container has zero accounts — if every admin is ever deleted, leaving these set lets a restart re-create this account as a recovery option, so consider clearing both fields once you've confirmed login rather than leaving your password sitting here long-term (this field is not real secret storage — see the password field's description).

Config.Name: BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password for the BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME account above. Masked in this form, but still stored in plaintext in this template's config and in `docker inspect` output — treat it as you would any other plaintext container env var, not as secure secret storage. Clear this (and the username above) once you've logged in and confirmed access, unless you want to keep it as an emergency re-bootstrap option.

Config.Name: RATELIMIT_PER_MINUTE
Config.Description: New SSH connections per minute allowed per source IP.

Config.Name: RATELIMIT_BURST
Config.Description: Burst allowance above the sustained connection rate above — 5 supports a user opening a couple of sessions in quick succession (e.g. PHONE plus a second window) without tripping the limiter.

Config.Name: RATELIMIT_MAX_IPS
Config.Description: Number of source IPs tracked simultaneously for rate limiting.

Config.Name: AUTH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
Config.Description: Seconds an unauthenticated connection has to complete login before being closed. 0 disables the timeout.

Config.Name: PENDING_EXPIRY_DAYS
Config.Description: Days before an unreviewed pending account (open-with-approval mode) auto-expires. 0 disables expiry.
